component.name: RadioGroup
component.description: Presents a list of options where only one may be selected. Wraps the Radio atom, shares a single `name`, manages selection, and cascades group-level disabled/invalid/required state.
component.note: Composite over the Radio atom. role='radiogroup' with an aria-labelledby group label. Declarative `options` + `onChange` API, mirroring Figma. Synced to Figma '❖ Radio group'.

tokens.radio: delegates all dial/label/state tokens to the Radio atom

useCases: Pick one option from a short mutually-exclusive set (plan, shipping speed, payment method)
Settings that offer a few exclusive modes

commonPatterns.name: Uncontrolled group
commonPatterns.description: Provide options + defaultValue; the group tracks selection internally.

commonPatterns.name: Controlled group
commonPatterns.description: Parent owns the value; onChange returns the newly-selected value.

commonPatterns.name: Required with validation
commonPatterns.description: Mark invalid when nothing is selected on submit.

commonPatterns.name: Horizontal layout
commonPatterns.description: Lay a small set of short options in a row.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using RadioGroup when multiple selections are allowed
antiPatterns.reason: A radio group is single-choice by definition.

antiPatterns.scenario: Rendering loose Radio atoms without a group wrapper
antiPatterns.reason: Loses the radiogroup role, the shared label, and consistent name management.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use RadioGroup so the set is announced and grouped correctly.

antiPatterns.reason: The group has no accessible name for assistive tech.
antiPatterns.alternative: Pass label (renders + wires aria-labelledby), or supply aria-label/aria-labelledby.

antiPatterns.reason: Hard to scan; takes excessive vertical space.

slots.label: Optional group label (renders a legend and wires aria-labelledby)
slots.options: Required array of { value, label, isDisabled? }

interactions.select: Clicking or arrowing to an option selects it and deselects the rest
interactions.keyboard: Arrow keys move selection within the group; Tab enters/leaves
interactions.disabledOption: Per-option isDisabled disables a single radio; group isDisabled disables all
interactions.invalid: Cascades isInvalid to every radio (empty dials show danger border; a selected one shows danger fill)

behavior.controlled: Controlled via value + onChange; uncontrolled via defaultValue with internal state.

accessibility.grouping: All radios share one name; the group exposes an accessible name via label→aria-labelledby, or aria-label/aria-labelledby
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Arrow keys cycle selection; Space selects; Tab moves in/out of the group
accessibility.screenReader: Announced as a radio group with N options and the current selection

figmaDivergences.figma: documented as <RadioGroup options onChange aria-labelledby isDisabled />
figmaDivergences.implementation: matches that signature and adds value/defaultValue, isInvalid, isRequired, orientation, and a built-in label that wires aria-labelledby
figmaDivergences.reason: Rounds out controlled/uncontrolled usage and validation without changing the documented core API.

aiHints.context: Reach for RadioGroup whenever the user picks exactly one option from a small set and you want it grouped, labelled, and accessible. Prefer it over loose Radio atoms. For multi-select use Checkboxes; for long lists use Select.
neverUseWhen: Multiple selections are allowed — use Checkboxes
There are 6+ options — use a Select / Dropdown menu
It is a single standalone boolean — use Toggle or Checkbox
